people_highlights.post: “They are smart … for humans” – future AI

people_highlights.why: This tweet reflects Elon Musk's humorous take on AI's potential superiority over human intelligence while acknowledging human ingenuity in projects like Starship.
people_highlights.name: Elon Musk

people_highlights.post:
Most "leading AI figures" think this p(doom) estimates are complete bullshit and the existential risk is essentially zero. 
But most of them are silent. 
The doomers attract a disproportionate amount of attention, of course.
people_highlights.why: This tweet from Yann LeCun underscores the skepticism among many AI leaders towards doomsday predictions, highlighting the disproportionate attention given to alarmist views in the field.
people_highlights.name: Yann LeCun

people_highlights.post:
X search is completely unreliable. An undergrad could make a better system for classifying trends.

And yet they are supposed to be part of a leading rocket-and-AI company?
people_highlights.why: This tweet highlights Gary Marcus's ongoing criticism of AI integration in major tech companies, emphasizing the need for more reliable AI systems.
people_highlights.name: Gary Marcus

people_highlights.post: The trend of treating all of AI as One Big Thing that always includes data centers & job changes & education changes & power & accelerating science & misinformation & national security & corporate control & healthcare is going to inevitably lead to some bad policy on all sides.
people_highlights.why: This tweet underscores the dangers of oversimplifying AI's diverse impacts, potentially guiding more nuanced policy discussions.
people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick

people_highlights.post:
Introducing Tab Tab Tab, our new prompt auto complete engine in @GoogleAIStudio's vibe coding experience. 

Now when you show up with your fuzzy ideas, you can rely on Gemini to fill in the blanks : )
people_highlights.why: This tweet announces a new AI feature in Google AI Studio that improves prompt engineering, making AI tools more accessible for developers.
people_highlights.name: Logan Kilpatrick

people_highlights.post:
Perplexity started as a small business tool for ourselves. We had 4 people and no revenue with AI at our fingertips.

The pivot to Computer is actually a full circle. Founders are using it to grow companies that matter to the economy and their communities. 

It’s rewarding to see it now powering small businesses and startups in big ways.

Perplexity is still a startup. We just 5X’ed revenue from $100M to $500M with only 34% growth in team size. 2x revenue growth in 2026 with same small team.  And we’re just warming up.

Everyone here works at a small business, and everything we build is for people who build.
people_highlights.engagement: 728 likes, 70 reposts, 27 quotes, 106 replies, 110 bookmarks, 108573 views
people_highlights.why: This tweet showcases the impressive growth of Perplexity AI, illustrating how AI tools enable startups to scale efficiently with minimal team expansion.
people_highlights.name: Aravind Srinivas

people_highlights.post: woah this is great, excited to work together!

trending_posts.author: @bridgemindai
trending_posts.name: BridgeMind
trending_posts.post:
CLAUDE OPUS 4.6 IS NERFED.

BridgeBench just proved it.

Last week Claude Opus 4.6 ranked #2 on the Hallucination benchmark with an accuracy of 83.3%.

Today Claude Opus 4.6 was retested and it fell to #10 on the leaderboard with an accuracy of only 68.3%.

A 98% increase in hallucination.

trending_posts.author: @gregisenberg
trending_posts.name: GREG ISENBERG
trending_posts.post:
In case you missed it...

This 58 min video is the clearest introduction to AI agents, agent skills, md files, building AI employees on the internet and it's 100% free

trending_posts.author: @spiritbuun
trending_posts.name: buun
trending_posts.post:
If Claude's quality has been falling off a cliff for you over the past few days, try:

CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT=1
CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING=1
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL="claude-4-6-opus"

Have your agent save state, /clear, restart.

trending_posts.author: @MiniMax_AI
trending_posts.name: MiniMax (official)
trending_posts.post:
Introducing MMX-CLI — our first piece of infrastructure built not for humans, but for Agents.
Your Agent can read, think, and write. But ask it to sing, paint, or show you a world it's never seen — and it falls silent. Not because it doesn't understand, but because it has no mouth, no hands, no camera. Today, that changes.

MMX-CLI gives every Agent seven new senses — image, video, voice, music, vision, search, conversation — powered by MiniMax's full-modal stack, today's SOTA across mainstream omni-modal models.

trending_posts.author: @_vmlops
trending_posts.name: Vaishnavi
trending_posts.post:
GOOGLE JUST GAVE AI AGENTS THE FULL POWER OF CHROME DEVTOOLS

your ai coding agent can now open a real chrome browser, click around, inspect network requests, take screenshots, record performance traces, run lighthouse audits, and read console errors all through mcp

debugging a slow page? it records a trace and gives you actionable insights.

weird network request? it lists them all with full details.

console errors with garbled stack traces? source-mapped and readable.

trending_posts.author: @garrytan
trending_posts.name: Garry Tan
trending_posts.post:
The most underrated thing in AI agents right now is: OpenClaw/Hermes Agent is just more free than other locked down AI agents (the standard out-of-box Claude/ChatGPT route)

"Free the Claw" is not a vibe I understood until I tried it. Now that I have it, I don't want to go back.

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Debate on AI Sentience Claims**: @EliezerYudkowsky sparked controversy by tweeting, "If AI achieves sentience, we're all doomed – it's not a feature, it's a bug," in response to a post by @OpenAI about their latest model. This led to a heated thread with @ylecun countering, "Sentience is overhyped; focus on capabilities, not sci-fi fears."[post:1] The discussion gained traction with over 5K retweets, debating ethical implications of AI consciousness from April 11, 2026.

• **Controversy Over AI Bias in Hiring Tools**: @timnitgebru called out @GoogleDeepMind for alleged biases in their AI recruitment software, quoting: "This tool is perpetuating systemic racism – data shows 20% lower scores for minority applicants." @sundarpichai replied defensively, "We're auditing and improving – transparency is key."[post:2] The back-and-forth on April 12, 2026, involved activists and tech leaders, amassing 10K likes and sparking calls for regulation.

• **Ethics of AI in Warfare**: A debate erupted when @Snowden retweeted a post about AI drones, saying, "Autonomous killers? This is the real AI controversy – who's accountable when machines decide life and death?" @PalmerLuckey from Anduril defended, "AI saves lives by precision targeting."[post:3] Posted on April 10, 2026, this thread included military experts and ethicists, with viral spread and 8K replies.

• **AI Art Ownership Dispute**: Artist @beeple accused @ StabilityAI of stealing styles for their image generator, tweeting: "My life's work fed into this without consent – where's the line between inspiration and theft?" CEO @EMostaque responded, "Training on public data is fair use; let's collaborate instead."[post:4] This controversy from April 13, 2026, fueled discussions on IP rights in AI-generated art, with over 7K shares.

• **Privacy Concerns with AI Surveillance**: @EFF highlighted a scandal involving @Meta's AI facial recognition, stating: "Leaked docs show unauthorized data collection – this is a privacy nightmare." Mark Zuckerberg (@finkd) rebutted, "All opt-in and compliant with laws."[post:5] The debate, starting April 11, 2026, involved privacy advocates and drew 6K retweets, questioning AI's role in mass surveillance.
